title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Tag Digest Reference
summary: A release team must prove exactly which immutable multi-layer artifact ran even after a mutable tag moves in the registry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker tag digest reference increment in image identity and layer provenance ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Tag Digest Reference · Theory Docker Tag Digest Reference
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Tag Digest Reference contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs. A defensible solution applies Docker Tag Digest Reference, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to image identity and layer provenance ledger.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-3
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: worked docker images layers registries 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A tag is an immutable content checksum suitable for permanent deployment identity. This fails the Docker Tag Digest Reference contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
